resources.string: Which Firebase service allows you to authenticate users?
Firebase Auth manages login, registration, and sessions.
Which NoSQL database are you using in I-Wivo?
Cloud Firestore is a cloud NoSQL database.
Which method is usually used to update specific fields in a document?
update modifies specific fields in a document.
What structure does Firestore use to organize data?
Firestore organizes data into collections and documents.
What does a collection represent in Firestore?
What does a document represent in Firestore?
A document contains fields and values.
Which property lets you get the currently authenticated user?
FirebaseAuth.currentUser returns the current user if one exists.
Which field is usually used as the authenticated user\'s unique identifier?
uid uniquely identifies the user in Firebase Auth.
Which method creates or replaces a full document?
set saves data in a document and can create or replace content.
Which method gets a document only once?
get reads a document or query once.
Which listener handles success in a Firebase operation?
addOnSuccessListener runs when the operation finishes successfully.
Which listener handles errors in a Firebase operation?
Which method allows you to run a transaction in Firestore?
runTransaction allows atomic reads and writes.
What is a Firestore transaction used for?
Safely updating data after reading its previous value
A transaction helps when the new value depends on the current value.
Which operation is suitable for adding coins while avoiding race conditions?
If the value depends on the current state, a transaction is safer.
Adds elements to an array while avoiding duplicates
arrayUnion adds elements to an array without duplicating them.
arrayRemove removes specific elements from an array.
Which Firebase service stores files such as images?
Firebase Storage is used for files.
Which service helps record app errors and crashes?
Which service allows remote configuration changes without publishing a new version?
Remote Config allows values to be enabled or configured remotely.
Where are Firestore accesses usually protected?
Security rules control reads and writes.
Which conceptual rule should be met to read private user data?
Private data is normally limited to the owner user.
What happens if auth.currentUser is null?
currentUser being null means there is no active session.
Which FirebaseAuth method signs the user out?
Which method is usually used to sign in with email and password?
signInWithEmailAndPassword signs in with email and password.
Which method is usually used to register with email and password?
createUserWithEmailAndPassword creates a user with email and password.
Which method sends a password recovery email?
sendPasswordResetEmail sends a password reset email.
Which I-Wivo field stores the user\'s XP?
Your structure uses the xp field.
Which I-Wivo field stores the streak?
Which I-Wivo field stores the last activity date?
Which field can store the user\'s visible name?
Which field can store a base64 profile image in your app?
avatarBase64 can store the encoded image.
Which field stores unlocked shop items?
Which field could store the equipped background?
Which field could store the equipped theme?
Which field could store the equipped effect?
Which field could store the last played topic?
Which field could store the last played mode?
Which field can store answered questions in the daily mission?
Which field can indicate whether the daily mission was claimed?
What problem is avoided by checking uid before saving data?
Saving data without an authenticated user
Without uid, you do not know which user document to write to.
What problem can appear when doing several dependent writes without a transaction?
Two simultaneous operations can overwrite each other.
What type of database is Firestore?
Firestore is a document NoSQL database.
What does it mean that Firebase is BaaS?
What should you avoid storing directly in Firestore?
Passwords must not be stored in plain text.
What is used for filtered queries in Firestore?
What is used to sort queries in Firestore?
What happens if Firestore rules are too open?
Risk of exposure or improper writes
What should you do with Firebase errors in the UI?
Show or handle them with onError
Handling errors improves user experience and debugging.
FirebaseAuth allows you to get the current user.
FirebaseAuth.currentUser returns the authenticated user if one exists.
Firestore stores data in documents and collections.
Firestore organizes data into collections and documents.
A transaction helps update data safely.
Transactions help when reads and writes are related.
If there is no session, currentUser is null.
Using !! on currentUser can cause a NullPointerException.
Firestore is a relational database based on SQL tables.
Firestore is a document NoSQL database.
set can create a document if it does not exist.
set can create or replace data.
update fails if the document does not exist.
update requires the document to exist.
addOnSuccessListener runs when the operation fails.
runTransaction can read and write atomically.
FieldValue.arrayUnion adds elements while avoiding duplicates.
FieldValue.arrayRemove is used to remove elements from arrays.
Firebase Storage is used to store files.
Storage stores images, videos, and other files.
Remote Config allows values to be changed remotely.
Remote Config changes configuration without a new version.
Firestore security rules do not matter.
Rules are essential to protect data.
A user should be able to read any other user\'s private data.
signInWithEmailAndPassword signs in with email and password.
That method signs the user in.
sendPasswordResetEmail can send a password recovery email.
It sends a password reset email.
email and uid are always the same.
uid is an internal identifier; email is a credential or data.
In I-Wivo, xp can be stored in the user document.
In I-Wivo, coins can store coins.
In I-Wivo, streak can store the streak.
lastActivityDate can be used to calculate the daily streak.
It allows comparing the last activity with today.
dailyMissionClaimed prevents claiming the reward several times.
It controls whether the reward was already claimed.
dailyQuestionsAnswered can count questions answered today.
It is used for daily progress.
equippedThemeId can store the equipped theme.
It stores the active theme id.
Storing plain text passwords in Firestore is a good practice.
Never store passwords in plain text.
They resolve through listeners, callbacks, or tasks.
You can assume a Firestore read immediately returns the data.
You must handle callbacks or coroutines.
A race condition can happen if two clients update the same counter without control.
Transactions are useful for adding XP or coins based on the current value.
They protect changes that depend on the previous value.
DocumentReference points to a specific document.
add creates a document with an automatic id in a collection.
document(uid) allows using uid as the document id.
This is common for user profiles.
If a Firebase operation fails, the error should be reported or handled.
Firestore requires every document to have the same fields.
Firestore is flexible; documents can vary.
NoSQL means you necessarily cannot query anything.
NoSQL does not mean there are no queries.
Firebase can save you from building a custom backend in a small or medium app.
transaction.update modifies data inside a transaction.
Complete the code:\n\nval claimed = doc.____("dailyMissionClaimed") ?: false
arrayUnion adds to an array while avoiding duplicates.
If there is no uid, it is better to stop with an error.
Complete the code:\n\nval data = ____("xp" to 0, "coins" to 0)
exists indicates whether the document exists.
toObject converts a document into a model.
email gets the user\'s email address.
Using uid as the user document id is common.
Passing null can clear the field.
Complete the code:\n\nval unlocked = doc.get("unlockedShopItems") as? ____
Complete the code:\n\nval ids = unlocked?.____<String>() ?: emptyList()
If there is no value, 0 is used.
Complete the code:\n\nif (currentCoins < item.price) ____ Exception("You do not have enough coins")
toString can store an ISO date.
lastActivityDate is stored as a String.
If it does not exist, it starts at 0.
If it does not exist, it has not been claimed.
A document stores fields and values.
Adds elements to an array while avoiding duplicates
arrayUnion adds elements to an array in Firestore.
Atomic operation with read and write
They control who can read and write
It can crash if currentUser is null
Using !! can throw NullPointerException if there is no user.
Detect the problem:\n\nI update coins by reading first and writing later without a transaction.
If several changes happen at the same time, a transaction is safer.
Detect the problem:\n\nI save user data without checking uid.
There may be no authenticated user
Before saving private data, you should check that there is a user.
The operation failure is not handled
It is better to use addOnFailureListener or an equivalent mechanism.
Detect the problem:\n\nI store passwords in Firestore as text.
Passwords must not be stored in plain text.
Detect the problem:\n\nI allow read/write to any user in rules for private data.
Detect the problem:\n\nI use update to create a document that does not exist.
update can fail if the document does not exist
To create a document, set is usually used.
Detect the problem:\n\nI read coins outside the callback and use it immediately.
Firebase returns data through callbacks or tasks.
Detect the problem:\n\nI use email as a public document id for sensitive data.
Email can be sensitive data; uid is usually better.
Detect the problem:\n\nI claim the daily mission without checking dailyMissionClaimed.
It could be claimed more than once
Which collection are you using for profiles in I-Wivo?
Your current structure uses the users collection.
Which field stores the user\'s coins?
Which field stores the user\'s streak?
What does auth.currentUser return if there is no session?
If there is no authenticated user, currentUser is null.
Which field stores XP in I-Wivo?
Which field stores the last activity date?
lastActivityDate is used for the streak.
Which field stores unlocked shop items?
Which method atomically adds a number in Firestore?
Which operation would you use to read a document once?
Which listener runs if an operation fails?
Order the flow to create a profile if it does not exist:
Get the uid of the current user
If it does not exist, call set(data)
First you need uid, reference, read, and creation if missing.
Order the flow to add coins with a transaction:
A transaction reads, calculates, and writes safely.
Order the flow to sign in:
First validate, then sign in, then navigate.
Order the flow to register a user:
Auth creates the account and Firestore stores the profile.
Order the flow to buy a shop item:
The purchase validates and updates the state.
Order the flow to equip an item:
Equipping stores the item id by category.
Order the flow for the daily mission:
Daily progress is updated according to the date.
Order the flow to claim the daily reward:
Add XP/coins and mark as claimed
The reward must be validated before being added.
Order the flow to load profile:
Profile depends on the authenticated user.
Order the flow to save a base64 avatar:
Firestore is the cloud NoSQL database.
A transaction protects reads and writes.
Clue: service for login and registration.
It runs when the operation finishes successfully.
It runs when the operation fails.
Clue: safe operation for adding fields after a previous read.
